rationale: The citing paper does not just list Shaddy and Fishbach (2017) as background; it reanalyzes that study as a central empirical illustration of the proposed mediation-evidence procedure. It reports the original mediation setup, compares conventional indirect/direct-effect testing with its Bayes-factor approach, and uses this specific case to show how evidence for full mediation can be quantified. The paper then interprets the reanalysis as providing substantial support for the mediation structure in that study, including causal ordering implications.
relation_description: Laghaie and Otter reanalyze Shaddy and Fishbach's mediation study to sharpen how strongly the data support full mediation. Their method adds an evidence metric (Bayes factor) and a clearer causal-model interpretation beyond the original significance-test presentation.
